stories.name: Basic Usage
stories.snippet: const BasicUsage = () => <StoryCard>This is a basic story card with default info status.</StoryCard>;

stories.name: title: provided
stories.snippet: const WithTitle = () => <StoryCard title="Card Title">This card has a title displayed as a heading.</StoryCard>;

stories.name: status: error
stories.snippet: const StatusError = () => <StoryCard status="error" title="Error Card">This card displays an error status with red background styling.</StoryCard>;

stories.name: status: warn
stories.snippet: const StatusWarn = () => <StoryCard status="warn" title="Warning Card">This card displays a warning status with yellow background styling.</StoryCard>;

stories.name: status: info
stories.snippet: const StatusInfo = () => <StoryCard status="info" title="Info Card">This card displays an info status with blue background styling.</StoryCard>;

stories.name: appearance: error
stories.snippet: const AppearanceError = () => <StoryCard appearance="error" title="Error Card">Use appearance="error" when the card conveys a failure or critical message.</StoryCard>;

stories.name: appearance: warn
stories.snippet: const AppearanceWarn = () => <StoryCard appearance="warn" title="Warning Card">Use appearance="warn" when the card conveys a caution or deprecation.</StoryCard>;

stories.name: appearance: info
stories.snippet: const AppearanceInfo = () => <StoryCard appearance="info" title="Info Card">Use appearance="info" for neutral context or tips (default).</StoryCard>;

stories.name: appearance: source
stories.snippet: const AppearanceSource = () => <StoryCard appearance="source" title="Source Card">Use appearance="source" for code/source blocks that should blend in.</StoryCard>;

stories.name: appearance: output
stories.snippet: const AppearanceOutput = () => <StoryCard appearance="output" title="Output Card">Use appearance="output" for results or success feedback.</StoryCard>;

stories.snippet:

This card contains multiple elements:

stories.name: className: custom
stories.snippet: const CustomClassName = () => <StoryCard title="Custom Styled Card" className="rbsb:border-2 rbsb:border-sky-500">This card has custom border styling applied via className.</StoryCard>;
